description:
You are acting as the “binary-curator” for an internal artifact repository.  
    The repository layout already looks like this:

        /home/user/repo/
           ├── releases/
           │      ├── binary_4.7.0.bin
           │      └── binary_4.8.1.bin
           └── stable/
                   └── binary.bin   (currently points to the *old* 4.7.0 file)

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Point the canonical link /home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin to the newest file (binary_4.8.1.bin) in the releases directory.  
       • If the link already exists, remove or overwrite it so that, when resolved,  
         `/home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin` targets the **exact** path  
         `/home/user/repo/releases/binary_4.8.1.bin`.  
       • Make sure the link is a *relative* symlink that reads: `../releases/binary_4.8.1.bin`.

    2. Create (or overwrite) a one-line log file at /home/user/repo/update.log that records the new link.  
       • The file must contain **exactly** the following single line including the arrow, one space on each side, and a trailing line-feed (Unix newline):  
         
         binary.bin -> ../releases/binary_4.8.1.bin⏎
         
       • No extra spaces, blank lines, or other text.

    Restrictions  
    ------------  
    • Work only with regular user privileges.  
    • Do **not** modify, rename, or delete the real binaries inside /home/user/repo/releases/.  
    • Use symbolic links (ln -s …) rather than hard links or copies.  
    • Ensure that the path separator and relative path spellings match the examples exactly, including case sensitivity.

    Validation checklist (automatic tests will verify):  
    1. `/home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin` exists and is a symbolic link.  
    2. `readlink -f /home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin` resolves to `/home/user/repo/releases/binary_4.8.1.bin`.  
    3. The link itself is stored as the *relative* path `../releases/binary_4.8.1.bin` (checked via `readlink /home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin`).  
    4. `/home/user/repo/update.log` exists, is a regular file, has mode 644 or looser, and its entire contents are exactly the single line shown above followed by a newline.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing state before the agent starts -->
    Directory /home/user/repo/releases/ exists and already contains two binary files:
        binary_4.7.0.bin  (dummy data)
        binary_4.8.1.bin  (dummy data)

    Directory /home/user/repo/stable/ exists and contains an outdated symlink:
        binary.bin  →  ../releases/binary_4.7.0.bin

    <!-- Expected final state for verification -->
    1. The file /home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin must be a symlink whose link text is exactly
         ../releases/binary_4.8.1.bin
       and whose canonical (absolute) resolution is
         /home/user/repo/releases/binary_4.8.1.bin

    2. A plain-text file /home/user/repo/update.log must exist with octal permissions 644 (or more permissive)
       and must contain exactly:
         binary.bin -> ../releases/binary_4.8.1.bin\n
       (one trailing newline, no additional characters).

    3. The original binaries in /home/user/repo/releases/ must remain unaltered.

    Automated tests will perform:
       stat/readlink/sha256sum checks on /home/user/repo/stable/binary.bin
       diff on /home/user/repo/update.log
